entry.title: Configure a tile on SAP Build Work Zone to manage Decision artifacts
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog post, you will learn how to configure a tile on SAP Build Work Zone to manage decision artifact within a business process project in the new Studio Home application. You can use this application to access business process projects outside of the lobby and allow business users to manage specific decision in the project without viewing or affecting the other project artifacts.

Empowering business to manage Decisions in SAP Build Process Automation.

You need to create a tile on SAP Build Work Zone for every shared environment where you have deployed your business process projects.

Prerequisites

  • You have created at least one Decision artifact in a business process project. For more information, see  Create a Decision .
  • You would need to access the decision and should have the Process Automation Developer roles
  • You also have the edit or view authorization for that specific project

Procedure

  • Step 1:  Create a Service Instance

You have to create a new service instance for SAP Build Process Automation. The creation of the new service instance will have the app-host-ids of the studio home and decision applications. It should have more than 8 app-host-ids. For more information, see Create a Service Instance .  

  • Step 2: Configure SAP Build Process Automation Destinations.

You have to create a new destination for the service instance that contains the app_host_Id for the application. For more information, see Configure SAP Build Process Automation Destinations

Note: Ensure that you do not have any two destinations in a subaccount with sap.cloud.service = com.sap.spa.processautomation or sap.cloud.service.alias = spa properties. 

  • Step 3: Refresh the channel manager

    • Open the SAP Build Work Zone Admin Console.
    • Navigate to Channel Manager tab to the left and press the refresh button available on the page. The refresh button is selected to update the list of HTML5 Apps in the Content Manager. Once the refresh button is pressed you will be able to view the Studio Home app in the Content Manager.

  • Step 4: Create a Local Copy of Studio Home

Step 4.1: Navigate to Content Manager tab and click on the Content Explorer button to go to the Content Explorer  page which will have the Html5 Apps tile.

Step 4.2: Click on the Html5 apps tile which will open the list of Html5 Apps. Search for the Studio Home App and Select and click Add button to add the application to your subaccount. If you do not see the Studio Home application refer to the troubleshooting.

Step 4.3: Open the Studio Home application in the Content Manager choose Create a local copy

Step 4.4: Choose Edit and adapt the texts in the General section. You can use a custom title and description for the tile.

Note: optionally you can adapt translation by navigating to the Translation Tab.

Step 4.5: In the Parameters section, enter the following values:

Name
Default Value
Id

Enter the project identifier.

Note: You can find the project identifier on the General tab in the Project Properties in the Lobby. In the business process project choose

 

 Manage the project properties.

modesaas
titleEnter a title for your tile
bpi.decision{"mode":"basic")
artifactTypesbpi.decision
environmentId

Enter the value of the Identifier.

Note:You can find the value of the identifier in the Lobby by opening the required environment from the list under the Control Tower > Environments tile.

UseUrlFullNamespacetrue

Step 4.6: Choose Save.

  • Step 5:Add the local copy of the Studio Home app to a group, catalog, or role

Add the local copy of the Studio Home app to a group, catalog, or role and make sure they are visible to users. You can create a role and assign to the user who can have access to this Studio Home app. You can add this application to the default Everyone role for all the users to access the Studio Home application. For more information, see Assign Apps to a Group and to a Catalog and Assign Content to a Role.

  • Step 6: Create and Navigate to a site in the SAP Build Work Zone Admin Console

Create a site if is not available for the user else you can use the existing site and assign the role which you created in the previous step for the Studio Home Application, by default Everyone role is selected and If you add the Studio Home Application to it, the application would be visible to everyone.

Result

Open the site and you will be able to the see the tile created for the Studio Home application under the group you had created.

 

Troubleshooting

The Studio Home App is not available in the SAP Build Work Zone.

If you do not see the Studio Home application in the SAP Build Work Zone's Content Manager, try the following troubleshooting steps:

Ensure that:

  • the service Key you created for the new SAP Build service instance has more than 8 app-host-IDs. Refer step1 for creating a new service instance.
  • you do not have more than one destination with the following properties:
    • sap.cloud.service = com.sap.spa.processautomation and
    • sap.cloud.service.alias = spa.

Refer step2 for creating a new Destination.

Error in Deployment

If the deployment of the project fails when you choose Deploy All without making any changes to the project, try the following troubleshooting steps:

  • The Deploy All feature deploys the entire project, including artifacts other than Decisions. The IT/ dev needs to make sure that the project is error-free so that the business users can maintain the decisions. If the project is failing with errors to the artifact other than decisions you can check the project in the Lobby . 
  • If the decision artifact is invalid, go to the invalid decision and check the console for any error that occured while maintaining the decisions. Once you resolve all the errors in the console, you can redeploy by choosing Deploy All.

Summary

You successfully created a tile for the Studio Home application to maintain the Decisions created without the use of Lobby. Using the tile you can also create a role and assign it to only specific users who can access the decision.

entry.title: Export Data to Excel in SAP Build Apps Using JavaScript and Flow Functions
entry.content:
In this blog post, we’ll walk through how to implement Excel file export functionality in SAP Build Apps using a base64-encoded string. This use case is particularly useful when you're working with data returned from a backend API in base64 format—such as when exporting filtered reports or datasets.

Upon applying filters and invoking the backend API, the response returns the Excel file encoded in base64. This string is then stored in a Page Variable, decoded using a JavaScript logic block, and converted into a Blob URL. The Blob URL allows the app to seamlessly trigger a file download, giving users a smooth and intuitive experience.


Step-by-Step Implementation

Step 1: Create a Page Variable

Create a Page Variable to store the base64-encoded string returned by your backend API.

Step 2: Add a JavaScript Block

In the Logic Editor, drag in a JavaScript block and pass the base64 string to it as an input.
Set the output of the JavaScript block to type URL (Any Protocol).

Paste the following code into the JavaScript block:

 

// Helper: create Blob URL instead of data: URL
function createBlobURL(base64String, mimeType = "application/octet-stream") {
    const byteCharacters = atob(base64String);
    const byteNumbers = new Array(byteCharacters.length);
    for (let i = 0; i < byteCharacters.length; i++) {
        byteNumbers[i] = byteCharacters.charCodeAt(i);
    }
    const byteArray = new Uint8Array(byteNumbers);
    
    const blob = new Blob([byteArray], { type: mimeType });
    return URL.createObjectURL(blob);
}

// Example usage:
const base64Data = inputs.base64Data;
const mimeType = inputs.mimeType || "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet";

const blobURL = createBlobURL(base64Data, mimeType);
console.log(blobURL);

return { result: blobURL };

This function takes the base64-encoded string, decodes it, and converts it into a downloadable Blob URL that can be used in the subsequent download logic.

Step 3: Install Required Flow Functions

From the Marketplace, install the following two flow functions:

  • Download Files

  • Export to Downloads

Step 4: Design the Flow Logic

Connect the flow in the following sequence:
JavaScript Block → Download Files → Export to Downloads

This setup ensures the decoded file is first processed and then downloaded properly to the user’s device.

Step 5: Configure the Download Files Block

  • Set the Download URL input to the output of the JavaScript block (the Blob URL).

  • Configure the filename and other settings as required.

Step 6: Configure the Export to Downloads Block

  • Map the inputs of this block to the corresponding outputs of the Download Files block.

  • This will save the file to the user's local device through the export mechanism.

Final Result

Once the user triggers the flow (e.g., by clicking a button), the backend API is called, and the returned base64-encoded Excel file is converted and downloaded — all within the SAP Build Apps environment.

This approach offers a clean, no-code/low-code solution to implementing Excel export functionality, even when the application is consumed via SAP Build Workzone there won't be any issue in exporting the file.

entry.title: New SAP Architecture Center: Foundation for Enterprise Architecture & Architects
entry.content:
The new Introduction from SAP, the  SAP Architecture Center is among one of the best addition to SAP's resources, making a centralized hub for SAP solution reference architectures. Through this addition, businesses can adopt SAP Solutions in a more effective manner, extracting valuable business insights from organizational data.

 

Key Features of the SAP Architecture Center

  • Comprehensive Reference Architectures: The Architecture Center provides a wide range of reference solution architectures, covering multiple business needs though technological domains and design patterns.
  • Technology Domains: It caters various domains which include Automation, Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Enterprise Integration, Governance and Security.
  • Technology Partners: SAP Architecture Center includes architecture involving major technology players like GCP, Microsoft Azure, AWS etc.
  • Interactive Exploration Tools: SAP Architecture Center users will be able to navigate the architecture through using tools like Overview, Architecture Explorer, Card-Style, and Navigator Style Overview
  • Community Engagement: For community interaction, there is a dedicated section, encouraging collaboration and knowledge sharing among the SAP professionals.

 

SAP Viewpoints

 

Architecture Explorer:

Through the architecture explorer you navigate through the Technology Domains like integrations, AI, App Development & more and Technology Partners like AWS, Azure and GCP, discovering solution blueprints based on your specific requirement like you are  planning an AI Driven use case on Azure or exploring data Architecture AWS, the Architecture Center guides through the most inline architecture assets with ease.

 

Technology Domains:

  • Application Development & Automation: Build and extend SAP applications using low-code tools, CAP, and event-driven architectures.

  • Artificial Intelligence: Integrate AI and machine learning into business processes with SAP AI Core and Launchpad.

  • Data & Analytics: Design unified data strategies with SAP Datasphere, BW/4HANA, and Analytics Cloud.

  • Integration: Enable secure and scalable integration across cloud and on-premise systems using SAP Integration Suite.

  • Operations & Security: Implement best practices for system security, observability, DevOps, and compliance.

 

Technology Domains:

The SAP Architecture Center focuses on the reference architectures across leading hyperscalers

  • Microsoft Azure
  • AWS
  • GCP

Even if you are using GCP for orchestrating analytics workloads, running BTP services on Azure or data Pipelines on AWS. The SAP Architecture center provides architectures patterns for each environment.

 

Reference Architectures:

SAP Architecture Center contains a rich repository of reference Architectures aligned with the recommended practices, well structured and reusable blueprints that elaborates how SAP Solutions are developed, integrated, deployed and scaled real world Enterprise Scenarios. Reference Architectures within the SAP Architecture Center is formulated in key sections, enriching the overall architecture in a more organized and easy to understand way. 


Conclusion:

Looking ahead, SAP Architecture Center will play a vital role driving standardized and future scoped solutions. As all the technologies are going through a continuous evolving process and towards a more modular, cloud native and AI Integrated solutions, and SAP heading towards the same,  many more things would be part of the SAP Architecture Center in the coming days which will accelerate digital transformations, ensuring architectural consistency. By adopting these reference architectures, your organization can future proof their Digital Transformation and IT Strategy with reduced complexity.

entry.title: SAP Developer News May 22nd, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/05/22/sap-developer-news-may-22nd-2025/ 

Live from the Sapphire Show Floor 

Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 1) 

  • SAP Joule Studio Blog post -  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/build-deploy-and-extend-ai-agents-with-joule-studio/ba-p/14105964?source=social-Global-LinkedIn-No-BTP-Awareness-Announcement-Video&campaigncode=CRM-YA23-SMS-1941773&sprinklrid=17083384815  
  • SAP Sapphire Innovation guide – BTP - https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/business-technology-platform.html 

 

SAP Architecture Center 

  • SAP Architecture Center online https://architecture.learning.sap.com/ 

 

Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 2) 

  • SAP Sapphire Innovation guide – BTP : https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/business-technology-platform.html 
  • SAP Business Suite Pricing and Packaging : https://www.sap.com/products/business-suite/pricing.html 

 

Sapphire Special Edition 

  • SAP Sapphire Innovation Guide: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide.html 
  • SAP Sapphire 2025: Notes from Day 1 Keynote with a focus on Developers: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/sap-sapphire-2025-notes-from-day-1-keynote-with-a-focus-on-developers/bc-p/14107827#M49882 

AI Updates 

  • Business AI Innovation Guide: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/ai.html 
  • AI Foundation: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/ai.html#new-ai-foundation-provides-an-operating-system-for-sap-business-ai 
  • Prompt Optimizer: https://www.notdiamond.ai/blog/launching-prompt-adaptation 
  • Perplexity.ai: https://www.perplexity.ai/ 
  • WalkMe: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/business-transformation-management.html#sap-introduces-new-walkme-digital-learning-solution-and-sap-business-suite-integration 

 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 Live from the Sapphire Show Floor 

2:21 Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 1) 

5:51 SAP Architecture Center 

6:52 Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 2) 

8:28 AI Updates 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for May 22nd, 2025. Special Sapphire Edition.

[Josh] Hey, everybody. Josh Bentley, Mr. Bluebeard, and representing the SAP Experience Center here on the Sapphire show floor. Now, what we've got is you come in and you learn about the different roles that you can do. You can be a CIO, CFO, different, different personas associated with all the technology SAP is talking about here at Sapphire. And once you do that, we create an AirTag that works with an Android or iOS phone. it gets custom built here and attached to your device so when you go home you can continue learning what you were actually exploring here on the show floor and if we go this way we've got the newest member of the sap community i want to show them to you he's AI powered by Joule i'm sure nice little robot there android and uh we got things going on with tons of business transformation AI uh supply chain over here we have an injection machine showing you how you can actually print the parts that were shown in the keynote and then this is how they come from the machine if it breaks you get the repair part actually from the warehouse so it's all automated and it's a great demo for all the customers here this is just one section of the Sapphire show floor i'm going to sneak out now the back and skip part of the experience center and go towards the learning and certification hub where everybody can actually go through and they can learn and take some tests on site and see what the certification programs are like on the way to that which is way back in the corner you can stop by any of the vendor booths that are happening and you've got plenty of vendors that we've known for years that are great trusted partners and customers and then we've got in the middle all the applications BTP all the sections that you can go to for let's say a different theater session I was talking to Sanya Leonard earlier and her team is doing great things with Abap cloud and so much is coming with that and AI and the innovations that we're doing in that space so again it's super busy here Sapphire This is the end of day three coming up, and what I want to get out of this is that you've got a ton of energy here in the community and a lot of things going on for not just developers, for everybody at SAP, but I'm heading back there to do some more work in the SAP Learning and Certification Hub. I'll talk to you later. Bye.

[Shrinivasan] Hello everybody it's an immense pleasure to bring you all the crucial highlights from SAP Sapphire 2025 annual conference that happened over last two days in Orlando Florida let's get this thing right off the bat Joule studio has been launched as part of SAP Build Solution Portfolio. Joule Studio has two key capabilities. Number one, Skill Builder Capability. Using this, you can design custom skills and execute predefined tasks within conversations triggered contextually by user intent. These skills produce specific results. They are configurable to the customer landscape. Skills can leverage other build components such as pre -built automations access to third -party integrations and AI models this feature will be made available by end of q2 this year number two agent builder capability using this you can create custom ai agents that can execute processes and workflows with varying adaptability and self-healing capabilities. Rather than following a set of prescribed instructions, agents leverage the thinking capability aspect of LLM to figure out how to solve a problem as well as APIs to augment their solution. Grounded in customer business data and SAP knowledge graph, these agents can deliver precise outputs and business-centric outcomes accommodating human inputs throughout the workflow. This feature will be made available Q4 this year. If you would like to know more details about Joule Studio, do read this blog post by SAP BTP expert Bharat Sandhu. Link is in the description box. That's not it. SAP announces new Joule capabilities for developers in SAP Build. These AI-assisted capabilities help empower developers at any skill level to build applications more quickly and easily. 1. SAP Build Work Zone Employees can work smarter with quick access provided by Joule to relevant apps, tasks, and processes from one central place. Users can further use Joule to query documents using document grounding capabilities. create new documents, and summarize them for relevant information needs. By providing AI responses based on SAP-relevant data from SAP Build Work Zone, employees can save time and stay productive. Number two, in SAP ABAP Cloud, whether it's migrating custom ABAP ECC code to SAP Cloud ERP to maintain a clean core or adapting existing legacy code to ABAP cloud model standards, these AI-driven enhancements can help developers be more productive and deliver precise outcomes faster. These two capabilities will be made available in Q3 this year. If you need more details, kindly refer to the BTP Innovation Guide provided in the description. Keep exploring and keep learning thank you.

[DJ] there's a new online resource for folks in the SAP tech ecosphere and that is the sap architecture center think of it as a one-stop shop for reference architectures on topics as diverse as event-driven application design multi-region resiliency federated machine learning and lots more why are reference architectures so important well think best practices think standardization scalability efficiency and interoperability and the best part about the new sap architecture center it's open open for collaboration for feedback and contributions using effectively the industry standard for collaboration on documentation and that is via GitHub. I've already made my first contribution. Dive in to the SAP Architecture Sensor now. See you online.

[Sheena M K] Beyond the exciting AI advancements around its supplier, SAP Build is bringing a host of expanded capabilities to help customers seamlessly extend their SAP business suite. Let's look at what they are. An enhanced Build Lobby which acts as the central hub for creating extension projects is now generally available which makes it easier than ever to get started. Extensibility Wizard, a guided tool which simplifies the process of building on stack and side -by-side extensions was made available in S4HANA public cloud edition at the end of last year. And here is more good news, by second half of 2025 this capability will also be available for SuccessFactors solutions. Data products from SAP Business Data Cloud will be accessible directly within SAP Build, supercharging application development while giving users richer data insights. This transformative integration is set to go live in second half of 2025. SAP Build is soon to support a wider range of event sources, enabling real-time dynamic interactions across multiple systems. Expect this feature to become generally available in the second half of 2025. SAP Build's streamlined pricing lets customers extend their business suite while giving developers the flexibility to choose the right tools without the hassle of managing multiple licenses. With these powerful new capabilities, SAP Build is set to transform how businesses create and scale extensions.

[Thomas] we've already heard from some of my colleagues about other ai enhancements that were announced at sapphire this week those affecting BTP the integration suite how Joule studio is being introduced inside of SAP build for extending Joule capabilities as well as how the business Data Cloud is the foundation of bringing business data into AI and making it more intelligent. But I'd like to talk to you now about some of the announcements that came that were part of the AI foundational layer. And right there, there was an announcement of a new version, new capabilities for AI Foundation itself. We see the current capabilities of AI Core and AI Launchpad being enhanced with new features like a new agent runtime. Or another example is the prompt optimizer to help you both optimize your existing prompts, but also help you write LLM independent prompts so that you're no longer tied with your prompts and your LLM generation overall to a particular LLM and that's through a partnership with Diamond AI. We'll be bringing the prompt optimizer to the BTP AI experience. We also saw another important partnership and that's with Perplexity. This is a tool that gives you an LLM-like experience but searching current information in the web. It's also really good at citing its sources and providing those in the output and this is an experience that we plan to bring to SAP Joule in the near future as well. So that was two super exciting partnerships that are going to enhance the AI capabilities of Joule and what you can build with AI on SAP BTP. And finally, we also saw the impact that the WalkMe acquisition is having to tie together Joule capabilities across your entire business, whether that's the SAP business suite or non-SAP capabilities as well. There's some really cool demos showing how ServiceNow was integrated with Ariba and S4. And Joule was able to tie that all together through the WalkMe capabilities. And like we said the other day in our little write-up after the day one keynote, This gives you a glimpse of some of the great things that are going to come to TechEd later this year because the announcements you hear at Sapphire are often the things we'll be able to show you exactly how you as developers are going to use later this year at TechEd. So be on the lookout for all that kind of information coming later this year.

entry.title: Fusion Forward: Unlocking Innovation with SAP Build – Series 1: What is Fusion Development?
entry.content:
In this post, you’ll learn how fusion development combines the strengths of citizen and professional developers using SAP Build and SAP Business Technology Platform to create fast, cost-effective, and business-aligned applications—covering the collaborative process, real-world examples, and how to get started with this innovative approach.

What is Fusion Development?

Fusion development is a game-changing approach that blends the expertise of professional developers (who code complex logic and ensure system scalability) with citizen developers (business users who understand processes but don’t code). By combining these strengths, fusion development creates applications that are both technically robust and perfectly aligned with business goals.

At its core, fusion development is about collaboration. It bridges the gap between IT and business teams, enabling them to work together seamlessly. With SAP Build, a low-code platform, citizen developers use drag-and-drop tools to design user interfaces, while professional developers handle integrations and backend logic using SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). The result? Custom applications delivered faster, at lower cost, and with greater relevance to business needs.

 

Real-World Analogy: Think of fusion development as a culinary collaboration. Citizen developers choose the ingredients—defining what the dish (app) should look like and do—while professional developers cook it, ensuring it’s perfectly seasoned and structurally sound. Together, they create a masterpiece that satisfies everyone.

Why Fusion Development Matters

In today’s fast-paced world, businesses need agile solutions to stay competitive. Fusion development delivers by:

  • Empowering Everyone: Non-coders, like store managers or finance teams, can create apps, reducing reliance on IT.
  • Accelerating Delivery: Apps that once took months can now be built in weeks, thanks to low-code tools.
  • Enhancing Collaboration: IT and business teams work hand-in-hand, ensuring solutions meet both technical and practical needs.
  • Maximizing Investments: For SAP customers, fusion development extends existing systems like SAP S/4HANA without costly customizations.

Example in Action: Imagine a retail chain needing an app to track inventory during a holiday rush. A store manager uses SAP Build Apps to design a simple barcode-scanning interface, while an IT developer integrates it with SAP S/4HANA for real-time updates. In just weeks, the app is live, streamlining operations and boosting efficiency.

The Power of SAP Build

SAP Build is the engine behind fusion development, offering a suite of tools designed for collaboration:

  • SAP Build Apps: Enables citizen developers to create intuitive mobile or web interfaces without coding.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: Automates workflows, like approvals or notifications, to streamline processes.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Provides professional developers with tools for backend development, integrations, and scalability.

These tools work together to create a seamless development experience. For example, a citizen developer might build a customer feedback app, while an IT developer connects it to SAP systems for data analysis—all within the same platform.

Benefits of Fusion Development

Fusion development offers transformative advantages:

  • Speed: A logistics firm built a delivery tracking app in two weeks, compared to months with traditional methods.
  • Accessibility: Retail managers create inventory apps, freeing IT for strategic projects.
  • Innovation: A startup prototyped a customer feedback app in days, adapting quickly to market needs.
  • Cost Efficiency: Pre-built templates in SAP Build cut development costs significantly.

What’s Next in the Series?

This is just the beginning! In the next posts, we’ll explore:

  • Real-world case studies, like automating invoice processing.
  • Step-by-step guides to building your own fusion development apps.

Get Started with Fusion Development

Fusion development is more than a trend—it’s a new way to innovate. With SAP Build, anyone can contribute to building powerful, business-driven applications. Ready to dive in? Join the SAP Builders community, explore SAP Build tutorials, or share your thoughts in the comments below. Let’s unlock innovation together!

Call to Action: Stay tuned for Series 2, where we’ll dive into a real-world example of fusion development in action. follow for updates!

Conclusion:
Fusion development is revolutionizing how businesses create applications by uniting the creativity of citizen developers with the expertise of professional developers. Powered by tools like SAP Build and SAP Business Technology Platform, this collaborative approach delivers faster, cost-effective, and business-aligned solutions. From empowering non-coders to accelerating innovation, fusion development enables organizations to stay agile and competitive in a rapidly evolving world. As we continue this series, get ready to explore real-world success stories and practical steps to harness the power of fusion development. Join the SAP Builders community today and start building the future of business applications!

Please feel free to share your thoughts in the comments section below.

If you enjoyed reading this blog post and would like to receive similar content in the future, please consider following my profile. By following my profile @gaddamarunkumar , you will receive updates on new blog posts and other content related to ABAP development best practices.

Follow the SAP BTP ABAP environment Topic page: SAP BTP

This page is a great resource for staying up-to-date on the latest news and developments in the SAP ABAP environment. You can follow the page to receive updates, post and answer questions, and read other posts on the topic

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What's New in Q2 2025
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP continues to evolve and expand, offering our customers improved resources and streamlined access to product updates. As we step into Q2 2025, we are excited to announce the addition of 11 new product pages. Read more about the details in this blog post.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Collaborating closely with SAP BTP experts, we've expanded the release coverage by introducing 11 new product pages. These pages compile links to all available release information, such as schedules, pre- and post-release communications, blogs, and more:

  • SAP Audit Log Service
  • SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service
  • SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment
  • SAP Connectivity Service
  • SAP Credential Store
  • SAP Custom Domain Service
  • SAP Destination Service
  • SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh
  • SAP Malware Scanning Service
  • SAP Private Link Service
  • SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI

You will notice that several of these pages share certain resources which are relevant across SAP BTP services that are relating to security.

Here's a glimpse at our new additions:

 

1. SAP Audit Log Service

This page contains specific resources for SAP Audit Log service as well as key release resources for SAP BTP security services, which also partially cover SAP Audit Log service among others. Here’s a list of what you can find on this page:

  • SAP Audit Log service
    • Release Schedule in SAP Note
    • What’s New Viewer: Release Notes
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Discovery Center
  • SAP BTP Security services
    • SAP Roadmap Explorer
    • SAP Community Page & Tag
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Security Products and Solutions Newsletter
    • Customer Influence

Check it out.

 

2. SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service

This page contains specific resources for SAP Audit Log service as well as key release resources for SAP BTP security services, which also partially cover SAP Audit Log service among others. Here’s a list of what you can find on this page:

  • SAP Audit Log service
    • Release Schedule in SAP Note
    • What’s New Viewer: Release Notes
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Discovery Center
  • SAP BTP Security services
    • SAP Roadmap Explorer
    • SAP Community Page & Tag
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Security Products and Solutions Newsletter
    • Customer Influence

Check it out. 

 

3. SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment

This page contains specific resources for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment. Here’s a list of what you can find on this page:

  • Release Schedule in SAP Note
  • SAP Roadmap Explorer
  • What’s New Viewer: Release Notes
  • SAP Community Tag
  • Documentation: SAP Help Portal

Check it out.

 

4. SAP Connectivity Service

This page contains specific resources for SAP Connectivity service as well as key release resources for SAP BTP security services, which also partially cover SAP Connectivity service among others. Here’s a list of what you can find on this page:

  • SAP Connectivity service
    • Release Schedule in SAP Note
    • What’s New Viewer: Release Notes
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Discovery Center
  • SAP BTP Security services
    • SAP Roadmap Explorer
    • SAP Community Page & Tag
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Understanding "My Processes" and "My Requests" in SBPA: Empowering Users Through Clarity and Control
entry.content:
SAP Build Process Automation is a low-code/no-code platform by SAP that enables businesses to automate workflows and business processes quickly and with minimal programming effort. 

As organizations increasingly adopt automation to streamline business workflows, user-centric features become essential for both transparency and efficiency. Two key views that support this in SAP Build Process Automation are: 
“My Processes” 
“My Requests” 

While they may sound similar, they serve distinct purposes in helping users and teams monitor and interact with automated processes. Let’s explore what they are, why they matter, and how they enhance the overall automation experience. 

 

Problem Statement: 

As organizations adopt workflow automation to streamline operations, users often struggle to track and manage their interactions with automated systems. Despite automation improving execution speed and consistency, lack of transparency and user feedback remains a major issue. 

 

The Problem: 

Users currently lack intuitive, centralized views within SAP Build Process Automation that clearly separate and present (a) the complex end-to-end business processes they participate in and (b) their individual service or task requests—leading to confusion, inefficient follow-ups, and reduced trust in automation. 

This results in: 

  • Uncertainty about the current status of submitted requests (e.g., approvals, service tickets, form submissions) 
  • Inability to monitor progress or identify delays in multi-step business processes 
  • Increased support queries to help desks or process owners asking for updates 
  • Low adoption or engagement with self-service automation tools due to poor visibility 

 

Why It Matters: 

  • Business users need a “My Requests” view to track simple, individual submissions quickly. 
  • Power users or process participants need a “My Processes” view to monitor end-to-end workflows they’re involved in or responsible for. 

 

The Need: 

There is a clear need for two distinct, yet complementary, views: 

  • “My Requests” – to provide users with a simple interface for tracking their personal submissions and their current statuses. 
  • “My Processes” – to allow users to oversee, participate in, and manage the lifecycle of complex, automated workflows. 

 

What are "My Processes" Views? 

“My Processes” application is dedicated to Process Participants like Approvers for example. This application would help the Process Participants easily track, understand, and make informed decisions about where their input is needed and how it fits into the larger process.   

 

Key Use Cases: 

  • Tracking the status of an employee onboarding process 
  • Monitoring a multi-level purchase approval 
  • Managing a customer service workflow 

 

Benefits: 

  • Real-time insight into the process lifecycle 
  • Accountability by showing who is responsible for each task 
  • Faster issue resolution when processes stall or bottleneck 
  • Ideal for process owners, managers, and workflow participants 

 

What are "My Requests" Views? 

"My Requests” View provides clarity to the Requestors on how their request fits into the workflow, the status of their request, and what actions (if any) they need to take as their request moves through the approval or fulfilment process.   

 

Key Use Cases: 

  • Viewing a submitted IT support ticket 
  • Checking status of a travel reimbursement 
  • Tracking a leave request form 

 

Benefits: 

  • Self-service tracking—no need to email or call for updates 
  • Simple, clean interface focused on the user's submissions 
  • Encourages user engagement with automation without needing to understand complex workflows 

 

Key Differences 

Feature 

My Processes 

My Requests 

Focus 

Entire process lifecycle 

Individual request status 

User Role 

Participant or process owner 

Requester or submitter 

Complexity 

Often involves multiple steps 

Usually single or few steps 

 

Prerequisites 

Here are a few Prerequisites for My Processes and My Requests View. 

 

Let’s explore the configuration details:

Configure My Processes Application 

Configure My Requests 

Now that both My Requests and My Processes tiles have been configured, let’s look at how we can visualise them: 

 

Entry Screen: 

 

My Requests view provides an overview of at all the requests initiated by you based on the filter criteria. You also have the possibility to look at the details of each request individually, the stage at which the request currently is in, the history of the previous steps, the status of the steps and the details of the approvers. You also have the possibility to look at all the tasks under a sub-process step (even if there is a hierarchy) and gain insights, without moving away from the lead process and much more. 

 My Requests

 

My Processes application provides an overview of all the processes that you have been given access to. You can also have a look at details of individual process instances and have the same capabilities as in “My Requests” view.  

 My Processes

Additional Key features are listed here. 

 

Conclusion 

As more businesses embrace automation, features like “My Processes” and “My Requests” ensure that automation remains accessible, visible, and actionable. By making it easier to track, monitor, and engage with automated processes, SAP Build Process Automation delivers not just technical power—but real business value. 

 

More Information: 

Configure My Processes Application 

Configure My Requests 

Use My Processes Application 

SAP BTP Innboytes blog post

This is only the beginning - stay tuned!

entry.title: SAP Developer News May 29th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

PODCAST:  https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/05/29/sap-developer-news-may-29th-2025/

SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Release 2505

  • Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-release-2505/ba-p/14102638
  • What's New in ABAP Generative AI in ABAP Cloud: https://help.sap.com/whats-new/4d359a7f8e8248e18f98e85ea0b9d83e?locale=en-US

Studio Home in SAP Build

  • Introducing Studio Home, new decision management application for business users

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/empowering-business-to-manage-decisions-in-sap-build-process-automation/ba-p/14092130

  • Configure Studio Home application in Work zone – SAP Help Portal

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/configure-studio-home-application

SAP CAP Developer Challenge June

  • Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/sap-community-escape-house-for-june-sap-cap-%EF%B8%8F-%EF%B8%8F/ba-p/14110094
  • SAP Community Escape House: https://sap-community-escape-house.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/

SAPUI5 WCAG 2.2 Update

  • WCAG Homepage: https://www.w3.org/WAI/
  • WCAG 2.2 Changes: https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/new-in-22/
  • SAPUI5 is Leading the Way in Accessibility with WCAG 2.2 Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sapui5-is-leading-the-way-in-accessibility-with-wcag-2-2/ba-p/14101596

 New learning content for SAP Integration Suite

  • SAP Integration Suite Basic Trial: https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/sap-integration-suite-basic-trial/ba-p/14112484
  • Start your free trial for SAP Integration Suite: https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/integration-suite/trial.html
  • Get Started with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh: https://developers.sap.com/mission.advanced-event-mesh-get-started.html

 

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Release 2505

1:29 Studio Home in SAP Build

3:38 SAP CAP Developer Challenge June

4:37 SAPUI5 WCAG 2.2 Update

5:18 New learning content for SAP Integration Suite

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for May the 29th, 2025.

[Shilpa] Hello, ABAP Developers. The ABAP release of 2505 happened last week, and I'm here to highlight some of the key features. Firstly, reduction in the technical downtime during the release upgrade, which is great to keep your business process running. Secondly, in ADT, there is a new wizard called Quick Fiori Application Generator, which will help you to generate a Fiori project within ADT itself. You need not have to log into Business Application Studio or Visual Studio Code to create a Fiori project, but it is integrated with ADT. A video about this feature will be available coming Monday in our SAP Developers YouTube channel. And thirdly and most important one, AI in ABAP. In the AI space, I want to highlight three things. First, Joule Chat is now integrated in ABAP repository object generator wizard, which will help you to generate OData UI service from the scratch just by using natural language prompts. Second, Unit test is quite important, and you can now use the prompt to generate ABAP Unit test methods. Third, the prediction of the code completion has been enhanced. There are other features related to ADT, OData, CDS, which is described in the blog post by Nora Klemp, which is in the description. Do check it out.

[Shrinivasan] Decisions in SAP Build allows business users to create business rules for approval policies, pricing logic, visibility check, etc. within a process automation project. Until now, even the simplest rule modifications required developer intervention, leading to delayed updates and a slow change management cycle due to their dependency. That's where Studio Home, SAP Build's latest decision management application comes in. With the new Studio Home, business users can now view, edit and maintain decisions from a dedicated user interface that only shows the decision artifacts within a project. Options such as inputs, outputs, reorder, create new rule are not present in this user interface, showing only the options that are relevant to the business users With a one-click button, they can release a patch version of the project and deploy it after making the rule updates This application comes with a design console that shows any error or warning message during the release and deployment of a project and a version history option to navigate and view the previous versions of the decision artifacts in the project. The Studio Home application can be configured to be accessed from a work zone tile. The instructions to configure the application in the work zone site is provided in the help portal. On top of Studio Home's release, there are enhancements to the expression editor in decision tables, making quick edits faster and more intuitive than ever before. With a new Studio Home, business users can now take the ownership of maintaining and owning the decision artifacts independently. By simplifying the process, reducing dependencies, and accelerating change management, decision management is more accessible and efficient than ever. Check out this blog post from Sesh Sreenivas to find out all the details about Studio Home and the enhancements to the expression editor for decisions.

[Ajay] Hello everyone. We have almost come to the end of this month and we thank all the participants who have already completed the ABAP Developer Challenge. We still have a couple of days to finish the ABAP Developer Challenge and earn a badge of completion. In June, we get to escape a brand new room for SAP Cloud Application Programming Model in SAP Community Escape House. This room will be unlocked next week for the participants to attempt the SAP Developer Challenge. In order to escape the CAP room, you would have to create a CAP project, create some custom code, and perform some testing. On your way out, you will also learn about remote debugging. In order to participate, you just need to use your user ideas and input and start the challenge and explore the story that unfolds. Links to the SAP Community's Cave House are included in the description. Wishing you all the best for your challenge and happy learning!

[Michelle] SAP has announced that its SAPUI5 Web Development Framework now complies with the latest Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, WCAG 2.2 and SAPUI5 version 1.136 and UI5 Web Components version 2.9.0. These updates enhance features like focus visibility and assistive technology, ensuring better usability for people with disabilities and improve the user experience overall. If you're interested to learn more of what kind of changes have been made to the SAP UI5 and UI5 Web Components frameworks, be sure to check out the link in the description below. Happy coding!

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. I'm always amazed by how accessible SAP learning content is nowadays. Long gone are the days that the only way you could learn about an SAP technology was by attending a course at a training center. I just want to highlight two new learning offerings that are available to all of you developers out there who are interested in learning about integration. There is the new SAP Integration Suite basic trial, which provides you a ready-made environment where you can get familiar with a few capabilities, like for example, cloud integration, API management, and integration assessment. It should take you around an hour 30 minutes to complete it, so go ahead and check it out. Also, there's a new mission in the tutorial system, which helps you get started with SAP Integration Suite Advanced Event Mesh. It covers everything, from creating an instance to connecting to an S4HANA cloud system, getting familiar with the main components of the service, and how you can integrate with other products, like cloud integration and SAP Build Process Automation. All the content is freely available. It is just a matter of dedicating time to learn. So, go ahead and enjoy the learning. Happy learning!

entry.title: Creating Local Entry Point for Business Solutions in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Creating Local Entry Point for Business Solutions in SAP Build Work Zone

High-level Overview (TL;DR)

In our previous blog, we introduced multi-tenant support for HTML5 business solutions using a central SAP Build Work Zone  instance. Now, we're excited to announce the new Local Entry Point (LEP) concept of SAP Build Work Zone that simplifies how providers integrate business solutions into SAP BTP. With LEP, providers can now automatically generate a standalone entry-point site on subscription without requiring a Work Zone instance on consumer tenants. This blog explains how to leverage LEP in your multi-tenant business solution.

Introduction

If you haven't read my previous blog discussing business solutions, CDM, and cross-subaccount federation of business applications, I recommend reviewing it first. It provides the context on the underlying concepts of CDM based content provider in SAP Build Work Zone. This blog assumes you understand these concepts and builds on them.

Since Q3 2024, SAP Build Work Zone supported multi-tenant entry points through a centralized instance & managed AppRouter, allowing consumption of HTML5 solutions across multiple subaccounts. Now, the newly released Local Entry Point (LEP) offers even more flexibility, enabling standalone entry points without requiring a Work Zone instance on the consumer side. Instead, the LEP mechanism automatically generates the entry-point site for subscribed SaaS solutions. This significantly reduces the administrative tasks required on the consumer side.

In a nutshell, Work Zone now offers a new service plan under the SAP Build Work Zone standard service (customers and partners). This plan enables SaaS application developers to use Work Zone as a managed reuse service that generates an entry point (standalone site) for the SaaS solution upon subscription in the consumer subaccount. This approach is similar to the existing SAP Cloud Portal Service (launchpad module) and serves as the successor for current usage of Cloud Portal Service in SaaS solutions.

In this blog, I will do a deep dive into how you can leverage LEP as a developer of multi-tenant business solution on SAP BTP, what are the provider subaccount and consumer subaccount administration tasks and finally compare the 2 approaches available in Work Zone to support entry points to SaaS applications.

Developer Flow: Local Entry Point

LEP is build on the principles of cross-subaccount federation of business solutions. Therefore, the sample pre-requisites hold good for LEP implementation as well - Work Zone content definition in cdm.json and ensuring all UI5 app manifests include the unique business solution identifier as sap.cloud.service name.

On a high level, the major difference to central instance based multitenant business solution federation are use of Standalone AppRouter and use of Work Zone standard local-entry-point plan as a reuse service to enable out of the box entry point which is generated upon subscription of the SaaS application on the consumer subaccount.

Pre-requisites

In the following technical deep dive, I would assume that you are familiar with SaaS development best practices on BTP and already have a multi-tenant CAP application where you intend to enable WZ Local Entry Point. In addition, verify the following before moving ahead - 

  1. MTA deployment descriptor is already generated.
  2. Standalone AppRouter is configured.
  3. Deployment configurations for HTML5 applications are in place & HTML5 repo (runtime & host) are added as resources in mta.yaml. 
  4. Every UI5 app manifests have the unique & uniform sap.cloud.service name.
  5. In the target deployment subaccount, ensure that there is an entitlement to SAP Build Work Zone service and service plans standard and local-entry-point.

If you do not have a multi-tenant CAP application already, and you wish to follow along with a sample application (completed code) - refer to this GitHub repo branch. I will be referencing this repo itself for all sample code in the rest of the blog.

Now, let's see detailed step-by-step approach to enable Local Entry Point. 

Assumption: You already have a mult-itenant CAP application & all pre-requisites are met

At first, 8 steps to enable LEP may seem very daunting, but I have broken down the tasks to smaller steps so that we understand what we are doing and why. I hope you will find the setup rather straight forward.

Step 1: Define Work Zone content in CDM format

This step is same as what was discussed in my previous blog. In order to access any Business Solution across subaccounts, the best practice is to expose the Work Zone content definition from provider (source) subaccount in CDM format and adding it as a content provider from consumer (target) subaccount. Same is true for Central Work Zone instance based access to SaaS applications - which are deployed in provider subaccount and consumed from the subscription subaccount - making it essentially a cross-subaccount access scenario. Although the consumer subaccount does not require a Work Zone instance or manual creation of content provider in the new Local Entry Point based approach, the underlying mechanism is still the same. It's just that the CDM based federation is automated under the hood, as part of the SaaS subscription flow and no additional administrative effort is required.

To define the Work Zone content as CDM, please refer to the SAP Build Work Zone documentation on CDM. You can refer to this cdm.json as sample and adapt it to your project requirements. I will not dive into the specifics of schema and how to create the cdm.json but focus on the 2 specific aspects around CDM for LEP - 

  1. LEP only support CDM schema version 3.1 or above and does not support Groups. Hence, the cdm.json should use spaces, pages or workpages.
  2. You must include one (and only one) site entity definition in the cdm.json which will be the default site for the Local Entry Point for the SaaS application and users would be routed to this site on the subscription side. This is also the entity where you can define the subset of site parameters which are usually available in the site administration view of Work Zone. For example, you can define the display config (site view mode, page width, etc.) browser setting (async loading, browser feature access, etc.), user capabilities (personalization, theme selection, locales, enterprise search, etc.) and so on. Given that there is no Work Zone administration capability on the LEP based solutions, these site properties are then provider choice and the only way to influence the Work Zone site behaviour that is generated on the consumer tenant upon subscription of the SaaS application.

Step 2: Setup cdm.json as deployment resource

This is a simple step to add a build script to mta.yaml which will copy the cdm.json from wherever it was added in the project path to the app deployment resources folder so that it's deployed to HTML5 repository and hosted for consumption as content provider from consumer tenants. Below you can find a sample build-parameters section in a mta.yaml and the lines 6 and 7 should be added in already existing mta.yaml - 

build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm ci
        - mkdir -p app/resources
        - cp workzone/cdm.json app/resources/cdm.json
        - npx cds build --production

Step 3: Add Work Zone LEP as reuse resource in mta

Local Entry Point approach is to use Work Zone as a managed reuse service to create a business solution specific Work Zone site on the subscription side without requiring an active Work Zone instance. Hence, it's imperative that we define this as a resource required by the SaaS application. This is done by adding it as an entry in the resources section of the mta.yaml as shown below:

resources:
  - name: incidents-build-workzone-service
    parameters:
      config:
        providerId: ${lepProviderId}-${org}-${space}
        exposureId: incidents.service 
      service-plan: local-entry-point
      service: build-workzone-standard
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service

Few important details to be considered - 

  1. You can choose any yaml-compliant name for the resource, but you would need this later to add dependencies / references from other resources / modules. Hence, best to select something meaningful and easily identifiable as WZ LEP resource.
  2. Exposure ID should be same as the sap.cloud.service (unique identifier for business solutions) that is used in every UI5 app manifest.json.
  3. Service Plan and service should be exactly as in the sample code above.

Step 4: Add dependency to LEP from AppRouter, AppDeployer & MTX module

Once you define the Work Zone LEP resource, it's time to make other components aware of it and hence we will add it as the dependency (requires) from AppRouter, AppDeployer & MTX module. In the mta.yaml, add the LEP resource name under requires for all the aforementioned modules -

requires:
      - name: incidents-build-workzone-service

Step 5: Add Work Zone LEP as required resource for MTX

In addition to adding the dependency in mta.yaml for MTX module, we would also need to add the SaaS registry dependency to Work Zone LEP service so that it can be used in a multi-tenant environment. This is done by adding a service dependency in the mtx/sidecar package.json. Go to mtx/sidecar/package.json and add the following - 

"requires": {
  "html5-host": true,
  "html5-runtime": true,
  "launchpad": {
    "vcap": {
      "label": "build-workzone-standard",
      "plan": "local-entry-point"
    },
    "subscriptionDependency": {
      "uaa": "xsappname"
    }
  }
}

Note that all 3 dependencies are required - html5-host, html5-runtime & launchpad. The vcap.label and vcap.plan for launchpad should remain as in the sample code above.

Step 6: Update the routes for LEP landing page in xs-app

In case of standard development flow and testing without dynamic tiles, custom domain, resource references, etc, following content of xs-app.json under the app/router folder should be sufficient - 

{
  "welcomeFile": "cp.portal/site",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ]
}

It basically defines the route to the launchpad / landing page which is shown when the user logs in to the Local Entry Point based Work Zone site.

Step 7: Update role template & add role collections for CDM roles in xs-security

Here there is a slight deviation to how roles are managed in cross-subaccount federation and LEP. When an Admin creates the roles manually based on a CDM source, the CDM roles are usually generated as BTP role collections with name in the pattern of ˜<content-provider-name><cdm-role-id>. But, in LEP, the role-collection is explicitly defined in xs-security.json (usually in the root of you MTA project) and it must match the role defined in the cdm.json. This is required for each and every role and there must be a matching role collection in xs-security.json corresponding to each CDM role. The matching role collections in xs-security can be added to the "role-collections" array as per following example -  

...
  "role-collections": [
    {
      "name": "Incidents_LEP",  
      "description": "Grant Administrative access to LEP",
      "role-template-references": [
        "$XSAPPNAME.Token_Exchange",
        "$XSAPPNAME.MultitenancyCallbackRoleTemplate",
        "$XSAPPNAME.support",
        "$XSAPPNAME.admin"
      ]
    }
  ]
 ...

Step 8: Deploy to provider subaccount, subscribe from consumer subaccount & test

This step is not specific to LEP as such and an obvious step for any development flow. In order to test the LEP based entry point, developers must deploy it to a dev subaccount which will act as a provider subaccount. Please note that currently, developers cannot test LEP based flow locally, which is otherwise supported in CAP based multi-tenant applications.

To deploy in Cloud Foundry, follow the standard mbt build followed by cf deploy <mtar-name>.mtar as explained in this tutorial.

Admin Flow

Provider Administration

The provider (app development team admin) manages the subaccount including adding entitlement to Local Entry Point plan for Work Zone standard service, adds trusted domains and monitors subscriptions.

As a prerequisite to deploy LEP based multi-tenant application on SAP BTP, administrators must maintain the entitlement to the Local Entry Point plan as seen here -

Once successfully deployed the service instances should show up in the respective BTP cockpit. Notice the Work Zone service can be seen as subscribed with plan as local-entry-point which should have 2 bindings as defined in MTA - MTX and the App Router applications. It should not be possible to subscribe to this solution from any subaccount and test the end-to-end flow.

You will also find the applications in the space where the SaaS application was deployed and perhaps a good place to observe that the AppRouter, MTX and CAP backend service are up and running.

On the provider subaccount, administrators can also find all subscriptions in the SaaS registry (can be accessed from Instance and Subscriptions in BTP cockpit) - 

Tenant specific route creation:

In development setup, in order to enable the subscriber tenant routes to access the provider content, Provider subaccount administrator must also create a tenant specific route for each subscriber. Please refer to this create tenant-specific route in Cloud Foundry guide. This route creation is usually done post every subscription and you would require the host name of the subscriber tenant to create it. The route should be mapped to the AppRouter application instance on the provider as well. Once done, users with required role collection assignment on the consumer subaccount would be able to access the Local Entry Point site.

Consumer Administration

Consumers have minimal administration effort: post subscription, they only need to assign users to role collections. No other Work Zone administrative tasks are required or possible.

SaaS application can be subscribed from SAP BTP Service Marketplace and once done the subscription should be visible in the Instances and Subscriptions in SAP BTP cockpit (see below for example). 

You can extract the URL to Local Entry Point either from this subscription link itself or from the button on the top right on the subscription details view (see above) - which you can then test or provide to users. 

Please note, this wouldn't work (404 Not found error) until the Provider Admin creates the tenant specific route as mentioned in the Provider Amin flow under section "Tenant Specific Route Creation". Once done, user should be able to launch the Local Entry Point site and navigate into the UI5 apps within the SaaS solution using the application URL provided the requisite role collections are already assigned - 

If you have keen eyes, you would have noticed that there's a UI integration card (declarative list type) in the site runtime screenshot above. I left it in intentionally as a teaser for what you can expect in the next blog 😉 

Central Instance vs. Local Entry Point – Key Differences

Now that there are 2 parallel options to achieve entry point for SaaS applications on SAP BTP using Work Zone, let us first demystify the options by comparing them side by side - 

Feature

Central WZ Instance

Local Entry Point

User access

Single business site for all SaaS solutions

Each SaaS solution provides its own business site

Consumer-side Work Zone Instance

Required (Standard or Advanced)

Not Required

AppRouter Management

Managed AppRouter (out of the box)

Standalone AppRouter (extensible)

Site Administration

Administered by Consumer; full Work Zone instance on consumer subaccount.

No Consumer Administration; Provider Managed as site properties in CDM

Layout Customization

Customizable by Consumers (administrator defines spaces/pages with federated business apps)

Layout Fixed by Provider (CDM-defined)
Groups are not supported

Based on these differences, one can the best approach to enable entry point to their multi-tenant business solution. 

Following 2 diagrams will help clarify the differences between these 2 options further, while presenting different subscription subaccount setup possibilities.

 

Conclusion

The Local Entry Point feature simplifies multi-tenant deployments, improving the flexibility and usability of SAP Build Work Zone integrations. Check out further details in the official documentation. Please review the current restrictions (updated regularly) to map your requirements before adopting Local Entry Point approach. Another excellent resource would be SAP BTP developer’s guide to implement multitenancy using Local Entry Point option.

Looking forward to your feedback and questions!

entry.title: SAP Build and ABAP Cloud Basic Trial Offering
entry.content:
Introduction

The Basic Trial offering for SAP Build has been extended with a new workbook called "Fusion Development with ABAP Cloud in SAP Build". Since SAP BTP ABAP Environment is part of the SAP Build offering we worked on creating a new workbook for the Basic Trial offering around SAP Build, that shows how developers can now create new ABAP Cloud projects, or work on existing ones, from the SAP Build lobby and then navigate to their ABAP development environment – the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse (ADT).

How to get your hands dirty

Simply start with clicking on the following URL:   

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/build/trial.html

In the following screen click on "Try Now". 

You will receive a user with a password that will allow you to authenticate at the landing page of the basic trial in the respective region for your user, e.g. https://trials.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/ .  

 

When having used the credentials presented in the web page you will get access to two workbooks that cover two different scenarios that are covered with our current SAP Build offering. 

The ABAP related workbook is named Fusion Development with ABAP Cloud in SAP Build.

Here it is described how you can build a side-by-side extension scenario using SAP BTP ABAP Environment when connecting to a pre-configured SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. 

 

⚠️Caution: 

Please be aware that it may take up to 30 minutes to fully activate your user account in the SAP BTP ABAP Environment.     

So we either recommend to start with the first workbook that does not have an integration to the SAP BTP ABAP Environment (which takes longer than 30 minutes 😉) or only start with the workbook until your user has been activated in the SAP BTP ABAP Environment. Sorry for this inconvenience.  

 

In the ABAP related workbook, you will learn how to create a side-by-side extension using the ABAP Environment of SAP Business Technology Platform. You will perform the following activities:    

  • Create a custom application using the ABAP RESTful application programming model on SAP BTP ABAP Environment as a side-by-side extension.
  • Enable the integration by creating OData client proxies to: a) trigger the creation of a sales order in SAP S/4HANA using the Sales Order (A2X) OData API from this application, and b) read product data using the Product Master Data Including Classification - Read OData API via a value help in this application

The resulting app will look like follows (see below). 

The app uses tha latest features of the RAP framework, namely background processing framewok (BGPF) and event drive side effects.

 

The above mentioned app uses a pre-configured SAP S/4HANA Cloud system as a backend system. 

In the SAP BTP ABAP Environement system a connection to the pre-confired SAP S/4HANA system will be used.

entry.title: Mastering Complex SBPA Approvals : A Deep Dive into Wait Steps, Script Tasks, and API Integrations
entry.content:
Welcome back to our new blog series,  our previous blog series  delved into the intricacies of dynamic sequential approval workflows.

In this two-part series, my colleague @Adwait_Fadnavis  and I will explore some very critical business workflow  requirements  in detail using SAP Build Process Automation. 

Throughout this blog series, we will delve into the following critical business requirements:

Part 1 - Current Blog :Mastering Complex SBPA Approvals : A Deep Dive into Wait Steps, Script Tasks, and API Integrations

Part 2 : Demonstration with Complete end to end testing 

We will demonstrate how to use various SAP Build Process Automation tools to solve real-time challenges effectively.

This blog post addresses this complex requirement with detailed step-by-step instructions. You'll learn how to ensure workflows advance smoothly, even with  approval condition utilizing various innovative latest features such as: Wait Step with API Triggering , Workflow Context update via API etc.

Business Context and Problem Statement

While working on multiple SAP Build Process Automation projects, we have faced situations where workflows only proceed if all departments or approvers approve them unanimously.

If any approver rejects the proposal, the entire workflow is rejected . This creates a need for a parallel approval process, adhering to an "all-or-nothing" requirement, which SAP's standard solutions do not support.

In a below sample procurement workflow, the request is created and submitted. Both the Department Manager and Finance Department must approve it simultaneously. If either rejects, the workflow is terminated.

If both approve, the request goes to the Executive for final approval. The Executive's decision determines whether the request is processed or terminated.

Dynamic Number of Parallel Approval
Technical Solutioning & Design

In this process, we will create two workflows using SAP Build Process Automation’s Low Code/No Code approach.

Let’s refer to these as Workflow A (main workflow) and Workflow B.

Workflow A( Main Workflow): This is the main workflow, triggered with all input parameters, responsible for the overall business process flow.

Workflow B(Secondary Workflow): This workflow handles the approval task. It receives the Approval ID, total number of levels, and current level. If the final approval is granted, it triggers a wait step API  in Workflow A to proceed to the next level. If more approvals are needed, it sends the Approval ID to the next level.

 
Technical Design Steps for Workflow A:
 
  1. Trigger Workflow: The workflow is initiated with input parameters.
  2. Decision Creation: A decision step is created to determine the number of approvals require. 

3. Script Task: This step determines the total number of approvals and the current approval level.

$.context.custom.doneapprovalcount = 0;
$.context.custom.currentcount = 0;
$.context.custom.subwfdefinationid = "Your Secondaray Workflow ID"; // replace with your secondary workflow id
$.context.custom.totalapprovalcount = $.context.decision_getAppprovers_1.Approvers.length;
$.context.custom.triggeredsubprocessids = [];
​
  • Condition Check: If the current level matches the total required approvals, A wait step will be created to wait for the completion. If not, Workflow B is triggered to initiate the approval task.

  • Trigger Workflow B: Workflow B sends the work item to the respective user for approval.
  • Wait Step API : This critical step waits for all parallel approvals to be completed. Once done, the dummy decision is triggered by Workflow B. This is very interesting and latest features
    1. Create a Wait Step with API as follows. After Deployment it will event will create the API End Point which will used to trigger from secondary workflow B .      
    2. Execution ID is the Parent Workflow ID.
    3. Upload the API specification and create the action for this
    4.  Also make sure to create the API key which will be used to trigger it from secondary workflow B.

Technical Design Steps for Workflow B  (Triggered via Workflow A)

  1. Trigger from Workflow A : Workflow B starts when Workflow A reaches a certain point to manage approval tasks separately and in parallel to the main workflow.

  2. Send Approval Task: The approval task is sent to the current level approver to obtain necessary approvals at each level before proceeding.
  3. On Approval: The system retrieves instance details and updates the approval count to keep track of completed approvals and update the workflow status.  
  4. sample code
$.context.custom.approvedcount = $.context.action_get_getV1WorkflowInstancesWorkflowInstanceIdContext_6.result.custom.doneapprovalcount + 1;
  • Check Count: The system checks if all required approvals are obtained:
    • If all approvals are done, the workflow proceeds to the next step.
    • If not all approvals are done This ensures the workflow only proceeds once all approvals are obtained
  •  Update Parent Workflow A Approval Action : increment the update count. created a custom action. If all the approvals not done, then update the approval count via custom action. (refer our previous blog how to create action for workflow API)   
  • Retrieve Task ID & Trigger Wait API trigger wait step ( API) in Workflow A to signal that the approval process is complete / Reject  and it can proceed.
    • Now come to most crucial part of the Development: Trigger the wait step via API
    • Created a custom action project  for the wait step api end point.
    • Here is the Input mapping for the API call. Needs to provide the API Key and Parent ID.
    • It will trigger the wait step in workflow A to approve / move forward the workflow . 
 
In the upcoming blog post - Demonstration with complete end to end testing  , @Adwait_Fadnavis  will delve into the complete end to end testing for these which is very critical to understand the whole development flow.
 
Stay tuned for a comprehensive guide that will enhance your understanding and streamline your Development  process.

Regards,

Mohit Bansal,

SAP Mentor. SAP BTP Solution Architect

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build: Highlights from SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
As the SAP Sapphire 2025 global keynote outlined, this year's focus was clear: AI, applications, and data. SAP Build continues to evolve in alignment with these themes, helping organizations efficiently build, automate, and extend their business processes and applications.

Here's a roundup of Sapphire's key SAP Build announcements and innovations to watch this year.

Joule Studio: Intelligent AI Agents and Skills Builder

Joule Studio Capabilities

SAP unveiled Joule Studio, a new environment within SAP Build that helps you create, deploy, and manage AI agents and custom skills. With Joule Studio, teams can enrich Joule with company-specific skills and intelligent agents that work across SAP and third-party systems. Joule Studio brings two powerful capabilities:

  • Joule Skills: Design modular, reusable skills that can be invoked during conversations. These skills can trigger actions, pull data, and orchestrate SAP Build components like workflows, automations, or apps.
  • AI Agents: Go beyond skills by building full-fledged AI agents that dynamically execute business tasks using reasoning capabilities. These agents use business context, data from SAP systems, and large language models to adapt to user needs.

Joule Studio will be available by the end of Q2 2025, with Agent Builder capabilities expected in Q4 2025.

Early adopters like Sovanta, an SAP partner, already use Joule Studio to enhance customer service. Sovanta significantly streamlined support processes and accelerated ticket resolution by integrating conversational skills with SAP systems.

Another early adopter, Pricefx - a global leader in cloud-based pricing software - is using Joule Studio to bring AI-driven intelligence into their solution. By developing an Agentic AI copilot, they aim to deliver contextual, business-aware guidance that enhances pricing decisions for their customers.

Interested in learning more? Register for the webcast: Meet Joule Studio: Drive Innovation with AI Agents & Joule Skills.

New AI Capabilities for Business Users and Developers

  • SAP Build Work Zone + Joule: Employees can now use Joule to find relevant apps, tasks, and documents faster. It can summarize or generate content based on your business data, saving time and boosting productivity.

Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

  • AI-Enhanced ABAP Development: Developers migrating to SAP Cloud ERP can use AI to convert custom ECC code into ABAP Cloud standards. This feature simplifies modernization efforts while ensuring code quality and compliance.

These capabilities are expected in Q3 2025.

 Expanded SAP Build Capabilities

SAP Build continues to grow as a unified platform with:

  • Extensibility Wizard for SAP Business Suite: A guided wizard that helps you build side-by-side extensions with the proper context, faster. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP SuccessFactors support is planned for release in H2 of 2025.

Extensibility Wizard in SAP Business Suite

Watch Sapphire Customer Session: Extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build at OGE: Open Grid Europe used SAP Build to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud—boosting onboarding efficiency by 50% and reducing invoicing time by 20%.

Watch Sapphire Solution Demo: Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud: SAP Build application development and automation. Discover how to rapidly personalize and extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while maintaining a clean core.

  • SAP Business Data Cloud Integration: Planned for Q4 2025, SAP Build will offer deep integration with SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) via CAP. Developers can consume and expose data products using virtual tables in SAP HANA Cloud, accelerating the development of insight-rich apps while reducing manual integration work. This improves data consistency and supports scalable, compliant development.
  • Event-Driven Applications: SAP Build, in combination with SAP Integration Suite and CAP, will support event-based interactions across systems. General availability is expected in H2 2025.

Simplified Pricing and Packaging

  • SAP Build offers a unified pricing model that combines app development, automation, and workspaces under a single entitlement. This simplifies adoption, reduces licensing complexity, and allows teams to build and extend at their own pace. Learn more in this blog post.

Thanks for reading! Which SAP Build innovation are you most excited about? Let us know - and don't miss these key resources from SAP Sapphire 2025:

  • Watch Sapphire Global Keynote: Your Best. Made Real. No Matter What Lies Ahead
  • Read SAP Sapphire 2025 Innovation Guide
  • Visit the Session Catalog with On-Demand Sessions

entry.title: Accelerate Every Stage of Your SAP Transformation with Enterprise Automation from UiPath and SAP
entry.content:
